notes: No consent wall encountered. Pages loaded cleanly via voidcrawl headless Chrome. networkidle timed out at 45s; omitting wait_for (default) succeeded at 60s — Yahoo hydrates aggressively.

price.notes: Quote Summary Panel price. Resolves on /quote/* pages; null on /markets/

change.notes: Absolute day change. Same scope as qsp-price.

change_pct.notes: Custom element. Resolves everywhere but is ambiguous on non-quote pages (grabs header ticker). Should be paired with data-symbol qualifier for strict extraction: fin-streamer[data-symbol][data-field='regularMarketChangePercent']

name_and_symbol.notes: Returns 'Company Name (TICKER)' string. Only present on quote page types.

fin-streamer[data-field=\"regularMarketP.notes: WRONG — grabbed BTC header ticker (first fin-streamer on page). Not safe without data-symbol scoping.

fin-streamer[data-field=\"regularMarketC.notes: Same ambiguity issue as regularMarketPrice without data-symbol.

[data-symbol].notes: Grabs header ticker element, not quote panel. Not safe standalone.

h1.notes: Site-level h1, not quote name. Discard.

results.transfer: PASS — all 4 fields resolved with zero selector changes
PASS — all 4 fields resolved with zero selector changes
PASS (unexpected) — quote summary panel persists across all /quote/* sub-tabs (summary, news, history, etc.). Selectors still work because the panel is embedded in every quote sub-page.

sample_values.change_pct: +0.83% (stray header ticker — not a quote-panel value)

results.transfer: FAIL (expected) — qsp-price/change/quote-title are null; page type boundary confirmed

scoring.transfer_rate: 4/4 fields on both MSFT and TSLA
caveats: fin-streamer[data-field=X] selectors are present site-wide but grab the FIRST matching element, which may be a header crypto/index ticker, not the quote panel. They MUST be scoped with data-symbol or a nearest quote-panel ancestor to be safe for extraction.
qsp-price / qsp-price-change / quote-title data-testid selectors are safe and scope cleanly to the quote panel.
The /quote/*/news and other sub-tabs also embed the quote summary panel, so the page-type boundary is quote/* vs non-quote pages, not quote/summary vs quote/news.

verdict: CONFIRMED: stable data-testid selectors (qsp-price, qsp-price-change, quote-title) give full domain-wide transfer across all Yahoo Finance quote pages with zero selector modification — discover once on any ticker, replay on every other ticker for free.
